note: extends to line end (vim C also deletes/inserts)
extends to line end (vim D deletes)
extends to line bounds (vim Y yanks line)
delete operator: fixed motion set only
change operator: fixed motion set only
yank operator: fixed motion set only
textobject via select-mode a base (operator-pending not chained)
textobject via select-mode a base (operator-pending not chained)
textobject via select-mode i base (operator-pending not chained)
textobject via select-mode i base (operator-pending not chained)
vim visual as: extend the selection by a sentence incl. trailing whitespace (textobject_sentence)
vim visual is: extend the selection by the inner sentence (textobject_sentence)
vim :goto is byte offset; zemacs goes to line
vim gU{motion} via gU/gu/g~ operator submap (motion subset: w/W/e/b/$/^/line)
vim gu{motion} via gU/gu/g~ operator submap (motion subset: w/W/e/b/$/^/line)
vim g~{motion} via gU/gu/g~ operator submap (motion subset: w/W/e/b/$/^/line)
spacemacs SPC t h h -> toggle cursorline
spacemacs SPC t i -> toggle indent-guides.render
spacemacs SPC t n a -> toggle line-number relative absolute
spacemacs SPC t n r -> toggle line-number absolute relative
spacemacs SPC t a -> toggle auto-completion
spacemacs SPC t w -> toggle whitespace.render all none
spacemacs SPC x a a -> align_selections
spacemacs SPC x d l -> delete-blank-lines
spacemacs SPC x d w -> delete-trailing-whitespace
spacemacs SPC x d SPC -> just-one-space
spacemacs SPC x i c -> change-case camel
spacemacs SPC x i i -> cycle-case
spacemacs SPC x i k -> change-case kebab
spacemacs SPC x i u -> change-case snake
spacemacs SPC x l d -> duplicate-line
spacemacs SPC x l s -> sort
spacemacs SPC x l u -> uniquify-lines
spacemacs SPC x t c -> transpose-chars
spacemacs SPC x t l -> move-line-up
spacemacs SPC x t w -> transpose-words
vim :g/:v via dispatcher hook; delete (d) and substitute (s/.../.../) actions
vim :g/:v via dispatcher hook; delete (d) and substitute (s/.../.../) actions

vim a] via c/d/y + i/a textobject (around)
vim a` via c/d/y + i/a textobject (around)

vim ap via c/d/y + i/a textobject (around)
vim as: a sentence incl. trailing whitespace (textobject_sentence); das/cas/yas
vim at: helix maps the object char differently (block-letter/tag/sentence)

vim a{ via c/d/y + i/a textobject (around)
vim a} via c/d/y + i/a textobject (around)

vim ib: helix maps the object char differently (block-letter/tag/sentence)
vim ip via c/d/y + i/a textobject (inner)
vim is: inner sentence text object — now a real sentence object (textobject_sentence); dis/cis/yis operate on the sentence under the cursor
vim it: helix maps the object char differently (block-letter/tag/sentence)
vim iw via c/d/y + i/a textobject (inner)
vim i{ via c/d/y + i/a textobject (inner)
vim i} via c/d/y + i/a textobject (inner)
vim '{a-zA-Z0-9} named marks (set/jump, edit-tracked via map_pos)
vim '{ named marks (set/jump, edit-tracked via map_pos)
vim `{a-zA-Z0-9} named marks (set/jump, edit-tracked via map_pos)
vim `{ named marks (set/jump, edit-tracked via map_pos)
vim m{A-Za-z} named marks (set/jump, edit-tracked via map_pos)
vim "{register} register select for next y/d/p
vim "``" register select for next y/d/p
vim & repeat last :s on current line
vim g& repeat last :s on whole file
vim ':set relativenumber' works via vim-compatible :set alias layer
vim ':set wrap' works via vim-compatible :set alias layer
vim ':set smartcase' works via vim-compatible :set alias layer
vim ':set wrapscan' works via vim-compatible :set alias layer
vim ':set textwidth' works via vim-compatible :set alias layer
vim ':set termguicolors' works via vim-compatible :set alias layer
vim ':set list' works via vim-compatible :set alias layer
vim ':set cursorline' works via vim-compatible :set alias layer
vim ':set cursorcolumn' works via vim-compatible :set alias layer
vim ':set scrolloff' works via vim-compatible :set alias layer
vim gv reselect last visual area (edit-clamped)
vim gi insert at last-insert position (^ mark)
vim . repeat-last-change (helix last_insert replay): insert-class changes (i/a/o/c + text) work; pure operator+motion repeat (dd, dw) is approximate
vim !{motion}{cmd} filter: linewise, pipes motion-covered lines through an external command via shell_pipe (!j/!k/!G); same key, same linewise semantics
vim !!{cmd}: filters the current line through an external command via shell_pipe
vim visual !: filters the highlighted lines through an external command via shell_pipe
vim visual D: delete the highlighted lines (linewise)
vim visual X: delete the highlighted lines (linewise)
vim visual Y: yank the highlighted lines (linewise)
vim visual C: delete highlighted lines and start insert (linewise change)
vim visual S: delete highlighted lines and start insert (linewise change)
vim visual R: delete highlighted lines and start insert (linewise change)
vim gx: open file/URL under cursor. Bound to goto_file, which opens URLs externally and files in-editor. Partial: gx in vim is URL-only (gf is files); zemacs gx==gf handles both.
vim CTRL-W H: move current window to the far left (swap_view_left)
vim CTRL-W J: move current window to the very bottom (swap_view_down)
vim CTRL-W K: move current window to the very top (swap_view_up)
vim CTRL-W L: move current window to the far right (swap_view_right)
vim CTRL-W R: rotate windows upwards (rotate_view_reverse)
vim CTRL-W S: same as CTRL-W s (hsplit)
vim CTRL-W c: close current window; zemacs uses C-w d (wclose)
vim CTRL-W h: go to window left (jump_view_left)
vim CTRL-W j: go to window down (jump_view_down)
vim CTRL-W k: go to window up (jump_view_up)
vim CTRL-W l: go to window right (jump_view_right)
vim CTRL-W o: close all but current window (wonly)
vim CTRL-W q: quit/close current window (wclose)
vim CTRL-W r: rotate windows downwards (rotate_view)
vim CTRL-W s: split window horizontally (hsplit)
vim CTRL-W v: split window vertically (vsplit)
vim CTRL-W w: cycle to next window (rotate_view)
vim CTRL-W x: exchange current window with the next (transpose_view)
vim CTRL-W <Down>: go to window down (jump_view_down)
vim CTRL-W <Up>: go to window up (jump_view_up)
vim CTRL-W <Left>: go to window left (jump_view_left)
vim CTRL-W <Right>: go to window right (jump_view_right)
vim [c: go back to start of previous change/diff hunk (goto_prev_change)
vim [f: same as gf: open the file under the cursor (goto_file)
vim ]c: go forward to start of next change/diff hunk (goto_next_change)
vim ]f: same as gf: open the file under the cursor (goto_file)
vim ga: print the ascii/unicode value of the character under the cursor (bound to :character-info)
vim gn: select the next match of the last search pattern (search_next). Partial: vim's operator-pending cgn/dgn combos approximate via the selection.
vim gN: select the previous match of the last search pattern (search_prev). Partial: cgN/dgN approximate via the selection.
vim gq{motion}: reformat text. zemacs reformats the motion's lines via the LSP formatter (vim uses formatprg/textwidth); same intent, different mechanism.
vim gw{motion}: reformat text and keep cursor; via LSP formatter (partial, like gq).
vim visual gq: format the highlighted lines via the LSP formatter (partial: not textwidth wrapping)
vim (: cursor N sentences backward (move_sentence_backward); faithful vim sentence boundaries (.!? + closers + ws, blank line)
vim ): cursor N sentences forward (move_sentence_forward); faithful vim sentence boundaries (.!? + closers + ws, blank line)
vim {count}%: jump to {count} percent through the file, first non-blank (match_brackets_or_goto_percent; %% with no count still matches brackets)
vim R: Replace mode (overtype). Typed chars overwrite existing ones, append at EOL. Partial: backspace does not yet restore the original overtyped characters (vim's restore-on-backspace).

vim visual CTRL-C: stop Visual mode (back to normal)
vim visual =: reformat/reindent the highlighted lines (format_selections, LSP-based)
vim visual O: move to the other end/corner of the selection (flip_selections)
vim visual P: replace the highlighted area with the register (replace_with_yanked)
spacemacs SPC w S: SPC leader -> hsplit
spacemacs SPC w V: SPC leader -> vsplit

spacemacs SPC p d: SPC leader -> file_explorer (approximate)
spacemacs SPC p k: SPC leader -> buffer-close-all (approximate)
spacemacs SPC p t: SPC leader -> file_explorer (approximate)

spacemacs SPC t V: SPC leader -> toggle line-number absolute relative
spacemacs SPC r e: browse registers in a picker (register_picker)
spacemacs SPC r r: show registers + paste chosen (register_picker)
spacemacs SPC r y: kill-ring browser (register_picker)
spacemacs SPC k j: tree-sitter sexp -> shrink_selection (approximate)
spacemacs SPC k k: tree-sitter sexp -> expand_selection (approximate)
spacemacs SPC k l: tree-sitter sexp -> select_next_sibling
spacemacs SPC k h: tree-sitter sexp -> select_prev_sibling
spacemacs SPC k $: tree-sitter sexp -> move_parent_node_end
spacemacs SPC k 0: tree-sitter sexp -> move_parent_node_start
spacemacs SPC k U: tree-sitter sexp -> expand_selection
spacemacs SPC k I: tree-sitter sexp -> move_parent_node_start
spacemacs SPC k w: tree-sitter sexp -> wrap_sexp
spacemacs SPC k v: tree-sitter sexp -> select_mode
spacemacs SPC k y: tree-sitter sexp -> expand_selection
find-file-literally -> open_file_literally (open then set_language(None): no syntax)
find-file-and-replace-buffer -> find_file_replace_buffer (open w/ Action::Replace, close old buffer)
marks register -> marks picker (jump to mark)
comment-to-line -> comment_to_line (prompt for target line, line-comment the range)
g<Tab>: last accessed tab -> alt file
zF: create a fold for N lines
CTRL-\ CTRL-N: leave insert to normal
CTRL-\ CTRL-G: leave insert to normal
CTRL-\ CTRL-N: stop Visual to normal
CTRL-\ CTRL-G: stop Visual to normal
jump to elisp function def -> goto_definition
jump to elisp variable def -> goto_definition
g,: newer change-list pos (approx last change)
describe-text-properties -> describe_text_properties (tree-sitter node stack + char at point)
search man pages -> man_page_search (apropos dynamic picker, renders man into scratch)
describe flycheck checker -> describe_diagnostics_checker (lang servers + advertised features)
verify flycheck setup -> diagnostics_verify_setup (lang servers + diag providers + counts)
U: undo changes on one line (approx undo)
z+: line below window (approx page down)
z^: line above window (approx page up)
SPC T s -> space T s
SPC T n -> space T n
SPC T p -> space T p
SPC h T v -> space h T v
helm-locate -> locate_file (dynamic picker over system locate, mdfind fallback)
invert comment to line -> invert_comment_to_line (per-line toggle over the range)

when 'wrap' off scroll screen N characters

when 'wrap' off scroll half a screenwidth

decrease current window width N columns

decrease current window height N lines
make all windows the same height & width

set window width to N columns

show first line found in current and
list all lines found in current and
list all lines found in current and

list all #defines found in current and

jump to first line in current and included
jump to first #define found in current and
jump to first #define found in current and
prepend to command to give a count
go to character at middle of the screenline
stop completion and go back to original text
accept selected match and stop completion
cursor to the first CHAR on the line of the
cursor to the first CHAR on the line of the
cursor to the first CHAR on the line of the
cursor to the first CHAR on the line of the
cursor to the first CHAR on the line of the

cursor to the end of last operated text or
cursor to the end of the current paragraph
cursor to the start of the current sentence
cursor to the end of the current sentence
display current file name and position
permanently mark word as correctly spelled
permanently mark word as incorrectly spelled
temporarily mark word as correctly spelled
temporarily mark word as incorrectly spelled
move to the previous misspelled word
repeat the previous ":" command N times
same as CTRL-V, unless used for terminal
search all open buffers (regex, not a fuzzy line picker)
goto_last_modified_file, not a full MRU picker
command_palette lists bindings (no dedicated maps picker)
commits log for current file -> git_file_log_picker
spacemacs spc_i_p_3 -> insert password for paranoids
spacemacs spc_x_i_cU -> change symbol style to UpperCamelCase
spacemacs spc_x_i_uU -> change symbol style to UP_CASE
spacemacs spc_x_i_x -> change symbol style to under_score
spacemacs spc_x_l_r -> randomize lines in region
spacemacs spc_x_w_r -> randomize words in region
spacemacs spc_w_x_sU -> horizontal split and focus
spacemacs spc_w_x_vU -> vertical split and focus
spacemacs spc_g_s -> git status (focus Git panel)
spacemacs spc_g_t -> git time machine (file history)
spacemacs spc_g_mU -> last commit message of current line
spacemacs spc_b_hU -> Help buffer (inline Help browser)
spacemacs spc_a_t_r_r -> ranger full layout (file explorer)
spacemacs spc_a_t_r_d -> deer single column (file explorer)
spacemacs spc_g_m -> magit dispatch (focus Git panel)
spacemacs spc_g_l_l -> browse to file at current line
spacemacs spc_g_l_c -> browse to file at a commit
spacemacs spc_g_l_lU -> copy link to selected lines
spacemacs spc_g_l_cU -> copy link at a commit
spacemacs spc_g_l_pU -> copy permalink to lines
spacemacs spc_b_u -> reopen most recently killed buffer
spacemacs spc_p -> open shell in project root
spacemacs spc_p_c -> compile project (run active config)
spacemacs spc_p_u -> run project (run active config)
spacemacs spc_p_i -> install project (run config manager)
spacemacs spc_n_r -> narrow buffer to selection (visual, via folds)
spacemacs spc_n_w -> widen (show whole buffer again)
spacemacs spc_k_c_c -> insert macro counter value, then increment
spacemacs spc_k_x -> go to other paren of the pair
spacemacs spc_k_i_2 -> switch to insert state
spacemacs spc_k_lU -> forward to next sexp
spacemacs spc_k_hU -> backward to previous sexp
spacemacs spc_k_iU_xinsertx -> begin of expression + insert
spacemacs spc_k_x_2 -> insert sexp before current
spacemacs spc_k_x_3 -> insert sexp after current
search with default input (symbol at point)
view file at branch/commit -> view_file_at_rev (git show <rev>:<file> in scratch buffer)
